name: Shortest Path (Dijkstra)
description: Dijkstra's algorithm finds shortest paths from a source in a graph with non-negative weights: distances start at infinity except the source, and a priority queue repeatedly extracts the closest unfinalized vertex, finalizes it, and relaxes its outgoing edges, updating tentative distances until the queue is empty and the shortest-path tree is complete.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Weighted graph (non-neg)"]
    N2["Init dist: src=0, rest=inf"]
    N3["Priority queue of vertices"]
    N4["Extract min-dist vertex u"]
    N5["Mark u finalized"]
    N6["Relax each edge (u,v)"]
    N7{"dist[u]+w < dist[v]?"}
    N8["Update dist[v], parent"]
    N9{"Queue empty?"}
    N10["Back to extract-min"]
    N11["Shortest-path tree"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|no| N10
    N10 -->|loop| N4
    N9 -->|yes| N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff

    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

Introduction to Algorithms (4th ed.)
